metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies the code-review region-gated floor is active under default config (e.g., a test or grep confirming the gate_dispatch / workflow_ops consumer path is exercised when novelty_drop_active defaults True).
findings.finding: The SHARED GATE deliverable — activating the code-review region-gated floor as a side-effect of flipping novelty_drop_active to True — is described in What but has no acceptance criterion that verifies the code-review floor is actually exercised or that its integration is unbroken under the new default.
findings.location: What section ('SHARED GATE — intentional cross-gate effect') vs. AC list
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] A test or explicit assertion confirms that with default VerifyConfig(), the code-review region-gated floor path in src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:608 (via gate_dispatch.py:720) is reached — e.g., an existing integration test that previously required explicit novelty_drop_active=True now passes under default config.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the stale-framing sweep to cover the Python function name `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` at test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:190 (and its section comment on :189), renaming both to default-ON framing (e.g. `test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes` / `# config keys (default-ON rollout + the back-out)`).
findings.finding: The rule-based stale-framing grep (as defined in the What section) will not catch stale default-OFF framing embedded in Python function names that use underscore spelling ('defaults_off'). Specifically, 'test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes' at test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:190 uses 'defaults_off' (underscores), which matches none of the grep patterns ('off by default', 'default[- ]off', 'default off', etc.). After the plan executes, this function name will permanently misrepresent the default as OFF to future readers, and the grep sweep AC will pass without catching it.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` → `test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes` and update the section comment on line 189 from `# config keys (default-OFF rollout + the back-out)` → `# config keys (default-ON rollout + the back-out)`. Optionally extend the normative grep pattern to include `defaults_off` to catch underscore variants in future sweeps.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` (and its section comment at line 189) to `test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes` (or equivalent default-ON framing) so the function name matches the post-flip assertion and is consistent with the updated section comment the grep sweep will already require.
findings.finding: The test function name `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` in tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py encodes a stale invariant ('defaults off') that will be permanently wrong after the flip. The plan's normative grep sweep (`default[- ]off|off by default|…`) matches only string literals, not Python identifier tokens; the function name is not caught by any specified AC or sweep rule and will survive as a misleading symbol in the test suite.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename the function to `test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes` and update the section comment from `# ── config keys (default-OFF rollout + the back-out) ──` to `# ── config keys (default-ON; set false to back out) ──` (or similar). Both changes are mechanical and require no logic edits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the stale-framing sweep grep (or its zero-hits assertion) to match only the two flags under change, e.g. pipe through `grep -iE 'remediation_mode|novelty_drop_active|rising.floor|region.gated.floor'` so correctly-False flags like `completion_floor_active` do not produce false-positive hits.
findings.finding: The stale-framing sweep AC requires `grep ... 'off by default|...|flag is False' src/ tests/ docs/` to return zero hits, but `completion_floor_active` is explicitly kept `False` by this plan and its docs/comments will (correctly) still say 'off by default' or similar — causing the blanket grep to return hits that are NOT supposed to be updated. The zero-hits AC will be unsatisfiable as written even after a fully correct implementation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 6; What — RULE-BASED STALE-FRAMING SWEEP; the grep command itself
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the zero-hits AC with a two-step check: (1) run the broad grep and pipe to a filter: `grep -rniE 'off by default|default[- ]off|default off|inert.*default|default.*inert|flag is False' src/ tests/ docs/ | grep -iE 'remediation_mode|novelty_drop_active|rising.floor|region.gated.floor'` must return zero hits; (2) separately verify `completion_floor_active` still reads `False` in _config_schema.py. State this two-step form in both What and the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the newly-defaulted code paths with default config end-to-end, or explicitly document why this is deferred (e.g. 'canary validation is owned by child ticket 49ae-258b-4534-4d83') — per E6(f), a cutover to a default path requires at least one criterion that exercises it as it runs in production.
findings.finding: The plan flips two flags to default-ON, routing all production traffic through the rising-floor and remediation-gate code paths, but no AC exercises those paths end-to-end under the new default config (live/real invocation). The updated test assertions (#4, #5) only check that the schema default value is `True`; `make test` covers existing unit tests that either use explicit configs or were already testing the True path in isolation. No criterion requires a smoke run against the live gate (or a documented rationale for its absence).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all bullets; E6 criterion (f)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'Rollback' sub-section (or a dedicated AC) that states: to revert, set remediation_mode=false and novelty_drop_active=false in the operator config and redeploy — and identify any observability signal to confirm the floors are inactive.
findings.finding: The rollback/back-out path for this behavioral default change is mentioned incidentally (once in the stale-framing sweep instruction as 'naming explicit false as the back-out,' once implicitly in AC#3) but is never stated as an explicit, standalone rollback plan, as required by T4.
findings.location: What section (stale-framing sweep paragraph) and AC#3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Rollback' bullet in the What section: 'To revert: set remediation_mode: false and novelty_drop_active: false in the operator VerifyConfig override (or environment variable equivalent) and redeploy. Confirm by checking that the rising-floor and region-gated-floor log lines are absent from the next review run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a staged-rollout specification (e.g., enable per-environment via config override first in staging/canary, then promote to default-on in production) or document why the existing config-override escape hatch is accepted as a sufficient substitute for a canary ramp.
findings.finding: The plan flips both defaults to True for 100% of traffic simultaneously with no canary or staged rollout specified. While rollback via config override is cheap and tested (AC #3), there is no mention of a phased traffic ramp, canary environment, or incremental enablement before full exposure.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or deployment note: 'Deploy to staging with default-True first; verify rising-floor and region-gated-floor metrics for 24 h before promoting the default change to production.' Alternatively, document explicitly that the operator config-override mechanism constitutes the rollback/canary control and reference the specific config key path operators should set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or name an existing test) that verifies the code-review region-gated floor (workflow_ops.py:608) activates correctly when VerifyConfig() is constructed with its new default (novelty_drop_active=True), covering at minimum the happy path through gate_dispatch.py:720.
findings.finding: The code-review region-gated floor (workflow_ops.py:608 via gate_dispatch.py:720) is newly defaulted ON by the novelty_drop_active flip, but the plan names zero test work or AC coverage for this newly-activated path — only plan-review test files are mentioned. If existing code-review unit tests use explicit configs or mock the flag check, the suite goes green without the live code-review floor ever executing under the new default, leaving a broken defaulted path undetected.
findings.location: What section (SHARED GATE paragraph) + Acceptance Criteria (no code-review floor AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '[ ] tests/unit/test_code_review_novelty_floor.py (or equivalent) confirms the region-gated floor is entered when a default VerifyConfig() is passed — i.e., novelty_drop_active=True — and exits correctly; explicit False config restores prior behavior.' If a suitable existing test already covers this, cite it explicitly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:189` (section comment `# ── config keys (default-OFF rollout…)`) and `:190` (function name `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes`) to the enumerated known-hits list, with prescribed updates to default-ON framing (e.g. rename section to `# ── config keys (default-ON + the back-out) ──` and function to `test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes`).
findings.finding: The plan's enumerated 'known hits' list for the rule-based sweep omits a stale framing hit in tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py at line 189: the section comment '# ── config keys (default-OFF rollout + the back-out) ──' matches the normative grep pattern `default[- ]off`, refers to remediation_mode, and needs to be updated to default-ON framing. The function name `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` (line 190) also carries the stale 'off' framing and warrants a rename. The informative enumeration lists only `:7` (module docstring) and `:192` (assertion) for this file.
findings.location: What section — 'Known hits as of HEAD' enumeration; tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:189-190
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Known hits as of HEAD' enumeration, add: `tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:189 (section comment 'default-OFF rollout + the back-out' → 'default-ON + the back-out') + :190 (rename test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes → test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes)`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
